January 1950; and then I will have to accept the thirteenth
degree of Virgo as the M.C. This is roughly twenty-eighth
minutes earlier then the given time. I cannot enter into the
question of alerting the given time to such an extent; nor can
I wait to see what the other progressed planets will have to
say, since at another time I wish to discuss the difficulties
involved in progression.
Let us consider another method. We are told by a very
good authority that the transit of any other planet across one
of the angles of the horoscope is very helpful in rectifying. The
angles are the most important and highly sensitive points in a
nativity; and the transit of any planet cannot be a matter of
dispute. lf a planet passes out of a positive quadrant (4th to
1st house) into a negative quadrant (first to tenth), then we
have an indication of passing out of the parent and the like.
For instance, an individual lost his father on 1911-11-18,
